Dashboard Archive: 2026-06-23 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows INSW and MIRM as the top two performers, both exhibiting strong sustained momentum across multiple timeframes. INSW ranks #1 with a composite score of 93.2, driven by exceptional 1-year returns of 124.05% and a 6-month surge of 86.99%, while maintaining all four SMA gates (30/50/100/200-day). However, INSW displays an "overextended" momentum signal (2.57) and shows significant ratio inconsistency—particularly a sharp dip in the ratio_1m_1w metric at 0.54—suggesting recent deceleration despite longer-term strength. MIRM follows closely with more balanced ratio progression (ranging 1.04 to 4.99) and a notably lower bad_spy_score (0.54 vs INSW's 1.09), indicating greater resilience during broad market downturns. Both tickers pass all SMA gates and significantly outpace SPY and QQQ on 1-week and 1-month timeframes, but the data suggests INSW may be experiencing consolidation after an aggressive run while MIRM shows steadier, more consistent momentum.
